title: Pre-Marital Masterclass
subtitle: Prepare for Marriage, Not Just the Wedding
description: A premium Christian course for engaged and seriously dating couples who want to build a wise, honest, and Christ-centred foundation before saying 'I do.'
bonuses: Relationship Red Flags and Wisdom Signs Guide
Money, Sex, and Power Conversation Guide

modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 1. Desire for marriage is not the same as readiness for marriage. This module helps you honestly assess your spiritual, emotional, relational, and practical readiness.
modules.memoryStatement: I pursue readiness, not just desire, before I say 'I do.'
teachingNotes: Wanting marriage and being ready for marriage are two different things.
Readiness includes maturity, teachability, honesty, and responsibility.
Marriage magnifies what is already present in each person — it doesn't fix it.
Unresolved issues do not disappear at the altar; they usually intensify.
personalReflection: Rate your emotional, spiritual, and practical readiness for marriage (1-10) and explain.
What might be a red flag we're overlooking?
What does true readiness look like for each of us right now?
coupleDiscussion: What area of readiness feels strongest for each of us right now?
What area feels weakest, and how could we grow in it before marriage?
What is a 'red flag' versus a 'normal difference' in our relationship?
modules.coupleExercise: Each of you writes an honest self-assessment of your own readiness (not your partner's) across four areas: spiritual, emotional, relational, practical. Share and discuss without defensiveness.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, give us honest eyes to see our own readiness clearly, not just our desire to marry.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, identify one area of personal readiness you will work on individually before the wedding.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A guided evaluation covering maturity, healing, spirituality, communication, and responsibility.
worksheetFields: Rate your emotional, spiritual, and practical readiness for marriage (1-10) and explain.
What might be a red flag we're overlooking?
What does true readiness look like for each of us right now?

modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 2. Not every strong connection is a wise covenant match. This module helps you distinguish between emotional attachment and wise relational choice.
modules.memoryStatement: I let wisdom, not just emotion, guide this covenant decision.
teachingNotes: Love should not silence discernment.
Character matters more than charm in the long run.
Peace and wisdom, not just excitement, should guide serious decisions.
Consistency over time reveals more truth than words in the moment.
personalReflection: What draws us together most: attraction, character, or conviction?
What warning sign, if any, have we noticed but not addressed?
What does choosing wisely (not just emotionally) look like for us?
coupleDiscussion: What character qualities matter most to each of us in a spouse?
Have we noticed any patterns of inconsistency in each other's words vs actions?
What would it look like to make this decision with both love and discernment?
modules.coupleExercise: Each of you separately lists 5 character qualities you see consistently (not occasionally) in your partner. Compare lists and discuss what patterns you notice, positive or concerning.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, give us discernment alongside our love. Help us see clearly, not just feel deeply.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, have an honest conversation about any warning sign either of you has been hesitant to raise.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A practical discernment sheet for serious dating or engaged couples.
worksheetFields: What draws us together most: attraction, character, or conviction?
What warning sign, if any, have we noticed but not addressed?
What does choosing wisely (not just emotionally) look like for us?

modules.moduleTheme: What You Are Bringing Into Marriage
modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 3. Nobody enters marriage empty-handed. This module helps you understand how family systems, past wounds, and learned patterns shape your future marriage.
modules.memoryStatement: I bring awareness, not just baggage, into this marriage.
teachingNotes: Family patterns often become marriage patterns unless consciously addressed.
Baggage ignored becomes baggage repeated.
Healing and awareness matter before covenant, not just after conflict arises.
Understanding your partner's family story builds empathy and realistic expectations.
personalReflection: What family pattern from your upbringing might show up in our marriage?
What baggage do you recognise you're bringing in?
How can we support each other in healing before marriage?
coupleDiscussion: What did conflict, affection, and communication look like in each of our childhood homes?
What family expectations or loyalties might affect our marriage?
What is one family pattern we want to intentionally not repeat?
modules.coupleExercise: Each of you draws a simple 'family map' listing key patterns (communication style, conflict style, roles, faith practices) from your family of origin. Share and discuss what you want to keep or change.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, help us bring awareness and healing into our marriage, not just unexamined family patterns.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, ask your parents or a trusted family member one question about their own marriage journey.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: Helps couples surface baggage and inherited patterns before marriage.
worksheetFields: What family pattern from your upbringing might show up in our marriage?
What baggage do you recognise you're bringing in?
How can we support each other in healing before marriage?

modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 4. What is avoided before marriage often becomes conflict inside marriage. This module helps you have honest conversations about money, intimacy, and power.
modules.memoryStatement: We discuss what matters now, so we're not surprised later.
teachingNotes: Money reveals values more than it reveals math.
Intimacy requires honour, clarity, and honest expectations before marriage.
Power must be understood through servanthood, not control.
Avoided topics before marriage usually resurface, often at a worse time.
personalReflection: What is our biggest unresolved conversation about money, sex, or power?
What expectation do you have that we haven't discussed?
What is one honest conversation we need to have this week?
coupleDiscussion: What are our individual attitudes toward saving, spending, and debt?
What expectations do we each have about intimacy in marriage?
How do we each understand leadership and decision-making in marriage?
modules.coupleExercise: Set aside 30 uninterrupted minutes to discuss finances honestly: income, debt, saving habits, and financial goals. Write down 3 agreements you land on together.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, give us courage to be honest about hard topics now, before the wedding day.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, have the money conversation you've been avoiding, using this module's framework.

worksheets.worksheetTitle: Money, Sex, and Power Conversation Guide
worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A guided workbook for difficult but necessary discussions.
worksheetFields: What is our biggest unresolved conversation about money, sex, or power?
What expectation do you have that we haven't discussed?
What is one honest conversation we need to have this week?

modules.moduleTheme: How You Speak, Listen, and Handle Tension
modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 5. Communication patterns established before marriage usually continue after marriage. This module helps you understand how you each speak, listen, and handle tension.
modules.memoryStatement: I will learn your communication language, not assume it matches mine.
teachingNotes: Tone often matters more than the actual words spoken.
Listening well is one of the clearest expressions of love.
Unresolved communication weaknesses become future marital conflict if unaddressed now.
Everyone has communication triggers that are worth understanding early.
personalReflection: How would you describe your communication style?
What tends to trigger misunderstanding between us?
What is one way we can listen better to each other?
coupleDiscussion: Do you process feelings out loud or need quiet time first? And your partner?
What's a recent moment we communicated really well? What made it work?
What's one thing you wish I understood about how you communicate?
modules.coupleExercise: Practice a 10-minute 'no interrupting' conversation: one person shares a feeling or concern uninterrupted for 3 minutes, the other reflects back what they heard before responding.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, help us listen as well as we speak, and understand each other's communication needs.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, notice one moment of miscommunication and revisit it together with curiosity, not blame.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A tool to identify relational tendencies before marriage.
worksheetFields: How would you describe your communication style?
What tends to trigger misunderstanding between us?
What is one way we can listen better to each other?

modules.moduleTheme: What Happens When Things Go Wrong?
modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 6. Every marriage will face conflict. This module helps you assess how you handle disagreement before covenant increases the stakes.
modules.memoryStatement: How we repair matters more than whether we ever disagree.
teachingNotes: Unhealthy conflict patterns should not be ignored during courtship — they tend to intensify in marriage.
Forgiveness and repair are essential to long-term covenant health.
The way a couple fights reveals as much as the way they love.
Escalation and withdrawal are both common but unhelpful conflict patterns.
personalReflection: How do you typically respond in conflict (withdraw, escalate, appease)?
What conflict pattern concerns you most?
What would healthy repair look like for us?
coupleDiscussion: What did our last real disagreement look like, and how did we handle it?
What helps each of us calm down and re-engage after tension?
What would a fair, healthy 'repair process' look like for us going forward?
modules.coupleExercise: Recall your most recent disagreement. Each of you shares what you needed in that moment and one thing you'd do differently. Agree on one repair ritual for future conflicts.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, teach us to fight fair and repair quickly, choosing reconciliation over winning.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, agree on a simple 'time-out and return' signal to use when a conversation gets too heated.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A review of how each partner handles disagreement before covenant increases the stakes.
worksheetFields: How do you typically respond in conflict (withdraw, escalate, appease)?
What conflict pattern concerns you most?
What would healthy repair look like for us?

modules.moduleTheme: Are You Building in the Same Direction?
modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 7. Marriage is more than emotional closeness — it requires spiritual alignment and shared direction. This module helps you evaluate unity in faith, vision, and calling.
modules.memoryStatement: We build our marriage in the same spiritual direction, on purpose.
teachingNotes: Shared faith should affect real decisions, not just be a background belief.
Misalignment in vision and calling creates strain later if unaddressed now.
Spiritual maturity and pace matter as much as shared beliefs.
Prayer and church life are tangible markers of spiritual unity.
personalReflection: How aligned are we spiritually right now (1-10)?
What shared vision do we have for faith, calling, and family?
What is one area of spiritual misalignment we need to address?
coupleDiscussion: What does our current prayer and church life look like individually and together?
What is our shared vision for faith in our future household?
Where do we sense calling or purpose overlapping, and where does it differ?
modules.coupleExercise: Each of you writes a short paragraph describing your personal sense of calling and spiritual priorities. Share them and identify one shared vision statement you both agree on.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, unite us in faith, vision, and calling. Help us walk in the same spiritual direction.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, attend a church service or spend a devotional time together intentionally as a couple.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A structured page for spiritual, relational, and family vision.
worksheetFields: How aligned are we spiritually right now (1-10)?
What shared vision do we have for faith, calling, and family?
What is one area of spiritual misalignment we need to address?

modules.moduleTitle: Setting Your Marriage Up to Win
modules.moduleTheme: Building the Foundation Before the Wedding
modules.welcomeText: Welcome to Module 8, the final module. Weddings are events, but marriages are structures. This module helps you create a concrete preparation plan that strengthens your future marriage, not just the ceremony.
modules.memoryStatement: We prepare for the marriage, not just the wedding day.
teachingNotes: Wise couples prepare intentionally rather than assuming things will simply work out.
Strong beginnings reduce avoidable pain later in marriage.
A foundation should be built deliberately before the covenant begins.
Pre-marital habits practiced now often become marital habits later.
personalReflection: What are 3 practical steps to prepare for our first year of marriage?
What boundaries or rhythms do we want to set now?
What does 'setting our marriage up to win' look like for us?
coupleDiscussion: What practical habits (finances, communication, prayer) do we want to establish before the wedding?
What boundaries do we want in place around family, friends, or work once married?
What is one thing we can start practicing now that will help our first year?
modules.coupleExercise: Together, draft a simple 'First-Year Marriage Blueprint' outlining 3-5 practical commitments (e.g. weekly check-ins, shared budget review, prayer time) you'll begin practicing now, before the wedding.
modules.prayerFocus: Lord, help us build wisely and intentionally, laying a foundation strong enough for the years ahead.
modules.weeklyActionStep: This week, begin practicing one of your First-Year Blueprint commitments together, even before the wedding.

worksheets.worksheetInstructions: A worksheet focused on the first 12 months after the wedding.
worksheetFields: What are 3 practical steps to prepare for our first year of marriage?
What boundaries or rhythms do we want to set now?
What does 'setting our marriage up to win' look like for us?
